title: Real-Life Story: Heather, Dental Hygienist
transcript: ﻿1 Industry Trends. The Health Science and Medical Technology sector is undergoing various changes which impact many affiliated occupations. For example, artificial intelligence is being used to automate tasks, improve diagnoses, and decision making, and assist with developing better, more personalized treatments. Personalized medicine is revolutionizing healthcare by enabling targeted, customized treatment plans that are more effective. These optimized plans are made by assessing a patient's unique genetic makeup, lifestyle factors, and specific disease characteristics. Another trend is telemedicine to deliver services over devices such as online consultations. Telemedicine has become increasingly popular, allowing patients to receive care wherever they are! title: Real-Life Story: Merritt, Mechanical Engineer
transcript: ﻿1 Welcome. We will be learning about the National CTE Sector, Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics, or STEM. The STEM sector covers work ranging from scientific research and technological development to engineering designs to mathematical applications. These fields drive innovation and address critical global challenges. The two main career pathways to enter this sector are Engineering and Technology, and Science and Mathematics. Common job titles include Aerospace Engineer, Anthropologist, Civil Engineer, Computer Engineer, Ecologist, Geneticist, and Meteorologist. "STEM is not just about learning facts and figures. It's about learning how to think critically and solve problems. It's about preparing our students for the jobs of the future." Says Michelle Obama, Former First Lady of the United States.

title: Meet Derek Carver, Entrepreneur
transcript: ﻿1 What's up everyone? This is Courtney Echerd back with Gladeo on the Go. Today I'm sitting down with super mom and owner of two businesses, Melody Godfred. Melody, thank you so much for hanging out with me today. Thanks Courtney. It's great to be with you. So glad you're here. So if you could just you a short little one sentence, what would you define your current occupation as? Sure. I'm a creative and I have two businesses. One is called Fred and far, which is a self love movement and the other is Write in Color, which is a career development company. Awesome. And I know that you went, you went college and law school and then had a lot of different jobs and then you created these two amazing companies. Can you tell me a little bit about that journey and how you got to where you are right now? Absolutely. For me, my career has really been about finding the through line where I can be authentic in my work. Amazing. I love what you say about, you have to be your authentic self to give your best contribution to the world, and I kind of feel like that's what your ring company is revolving, right? Yes. Being your best self and loving your best self. So can you tell us a little bit about that and where the inspiration from that came from? Sure. So Fred and Far has one core product, which is called the Self Love Pinky Ring. It's a ring that women wear as a symbol and reminder of their self commitment and their desire to practice self-love and self-care. Awesome. I love how both your companies really reflect on developing yourself and loving yourself. So I feel like Write in Color is a little bit more about developing yourself. Can you tell me a little bit more about how your resume company helps people? Sure. So our mission at Write in Color is to help everyone understand the strength and power of their unique narratives. So it's not just about putting on paper everything you've done in your career, it's about recognizing what the through line is, what your unique selling proposition is, the contributions you've made along the way, and aligning that with your goals. Okay. So you're an entrepreneur, you say you got there by accident. What do you think it means to be an entrepreneur and what's your advice for someone that might wanna do that? Sure. So what I think it means to be an entrepreneur is to be a creator, is to go out into the world and create something from an authentic place. And the way that you do it is simply by getting started. I think that we all feel like you need to have a business plan. You need to have all these resources, you need to have funding. And the truth of the matter is all you have to have is an authentic idea because that's what's gonna resonate with your audience. And then you need to execute on it and improve it every single day. So my advice to people getting started is minimum viable product. Figure out what is the easiest, fastest way you can get started. Because once you get started, your audience, the world will help you evolve your product and your messaging so that it can be the best possible thing. Wow. Well thank you so much for your words. I know that young women are really gonna benefit from them in whatever career they might take. So I really appreciate your time today. It's my pleasure. Thanks Courtney.

title: Meet Dr. Diane Beckles, Professor
transcript: WEBVTT - https://maestrasuite.com Can you think of a time in your life when you faced a major challenge or a failure or something that felt like a roadblock? I grew up poor, went to university in England. I, I was really lucky to, to be able to get these scholarships. I knew I wanted a PhD and that I would need another scholarship, and the pressure became immense. You cannot fail. You're on a scholarship. You cannot... You have to get A's, and on top of that, you have to excel in order to prove you can do a PhD, and you have to... There were all of these have-to's, and it became really overwhelming. I went to see the university counselor. At that time, that was something that was a source of shame for me because in my culture, you didn't go to a counselor. I had a scientific mind, and I recognized that I needed help beyond what I could figure out for myself. I think that was the scariest point in my life because there was a chance that I could may not achieve my goals. I just decided, I, I will just keep doing my best and then let the chips fall where they may.

title: Meet Mikaela - Pharmacy Technician
transcript: 안녕하세요, 저는 케이틀린입니다 여기는 Gladeo on the Go Zoom 버전입니다. 오늘은 Foothill College 졸업생이자 수의사 기술자인 Aileen을 만나보겠습니다. 그녀의 이야기를 들어보시죠. 안녕하세요, 저는 Aileen입니다. 저는 수의사 기술자입니다. 수의사 기술자로서 무슨 일을 하시나요? RN, 등록된 병원 간호사라고 생각해 보세요. 엑스레이, 실험실 작업, 예방 접종, 0 치과 선택적 시술 중성화 수술, 필요한 경우 절단, 필요한 경우 대량 제거를 합니다. 또한 애완동물의 여행 건강 증명서도 발급합니다. 여행을 하려면 법적으로 올바른 서류가 있어야 하는데 사람뿐만 아니라 애완동물에게도 필요합니다. 졸업 후 어떻게 이 직업을 꽤 빨리 얻게 되셨나요? 저희는 정말 수요가 많습니다. 안타깝게도 저희 같은 사람은 많지 않아요. 그래서, 아시다시피, RVT가 일자리를 찾고 있을 때, 모든 병원에서 "저를 낚아채고 싶어요."라고 말하죠. 당신은 이런 모든 경험과 지식을 가지고 있고, 병원에 있어서 큰 자산이 될 거라고 생각해요. 하지만 지금 직장에서는 일종의 도약을 했습니다 아시다시피, 저는 학교를 졸업했고, 일반 진료에서 모든 경험을 가지고 있습니다. 네, 응급이나 전문 분야는 아니지만, 제가 무엇을 할 수 있는지 알 만큼은 충분히 있습니다. 거의 즉시 채용되었습니다. 그들은, 아시다시피, 물론, 시간 노력을 그 분야에 대한 동기 부여와 열정이 있는 사람에게 투자하고 싶어했습니다. 그러니까 당신은 공식적으로 수의사 기술자군요. 항상 이것이 당신이 하고 싶은 일이라는 걸 알고 있었나요? 항상 수의사 기술자가 되고 싶었던 아니었어요. 어렸을 때 동물을 좋아해서 항상 주변에 동물들이 있었지만 그게 제가 하고 싶은 일이라는 걸 전혀 몰랐어요. 정말 친한 친구가 연락을 해왔어요. 야, 내가 수의사 기술자가 될 거야. 라고 했고, 저는 대체 그게 뭐지?라고 생각했죠 그래서 친구가 그 이야기를 해줬고, 저는 와, 정말 흥미로운 ."라고 생각했어요. 친구가 설명해 줬어요. 수의사 기술자는 환자와 더 많은 시간을 보내야 한다고요 수의사 기술자로서 환자와 많은 시간을 보내야 하는데, 저는 오, 정말 좋다."라고 생각했어요 이 직업에서 가장 좋아하는 부분은 무엇인가요? 지금 제 직업에서 가장 좋아하는 부분은 출근해서 팀이 준비된 모습을 보는 것입니다. 저를 든든하게 지지해 주는 팀이 있다는 를 뒷받침해 줄 뿐만 아니라 자신이 하는 일에 대한 열정과 사랑을 느낄 수 있기 때문입니다. 정말 큰 차이를 만듭니다. Foothill College에서의 경험은 어땠나요? Foothill 정말 훌륭했습니다. 제가 만났던 선생님들은 정말 배려심이 많았습니다. 미안합니다. 그들이 그리워요. 눈물이 나려고 합니다. 프로그램 디렉터부터 파트타임 직원 모든 선생님들이 정말 훌륭합니다. 학교에 없을 때도 질문이 있는 경우 이메일을 보내면 쉬는 날에 답변해 줍니다. 당신은 1세대 대학생이고, 그건 엄청난 성취입니다. 당신에게는 무슨 의미가 있나요? 저에게는, 엄마 그녀의 가족 베트남 전쟁에서 도망쳤다는 이유만으로 엄청난 압박감을 느꼈습니다. 그들은 배를 타고 여기 와서 생계 유지하기 위해 애썼고, 자녀들에게 최고의 삶을 주기 위해 애썼습니다. 그래서 저는 세대 대학생으로서, 엄마를 스럽게 만들고 싶었고, 엄마의 모든 노고가 헛되지 않았다는 것을 해주고 싶었습니다. 가족 중에서 처음으로 대학에 진학하면서 어려움은 있었나요 네, 그저 제가 원하는 곳에 도달하기 위해 어떤 단계를 거쳐야 하는지 알아내는 과정일 뿐이었습니다 제 가족은 대학에 가고, 취직을 한다고 했기 때문에 정말 힘들었습니다. 그럼 그 사이의 단계는 뭐죠? 그들은, 모르겠어요, 알아내라고 했습니다. 내 생각은, 오, 좋네. 수의사 기술자로 적합한 사람을 설명해 줄 수 있나요? 배우고자 하는 의지가 있는 사람, 학교에서 배우는 것 같지는 않지만, 직장에서, 직장 밖에서 계속해서 배우는 사람. 그들은 큰 마음을 가져야 합니다. 그들은 깨어나 인 태도를 갖도록 동기를 부여받고, 인내심을 갖고, 자신 자비를 베풀어야 합니다. 물론 사람들과 그들의 애완동물에게도요. 또한 훌륭한 리더십도 필요합니다. 아시다시피, 그들은 무언가 잘못되었을 때 목소리를 냅니다. 일을 주도적으로 합니다. 누구나 수의사 기술자가 될 수 있습니다. 그런 특성 있다면 이 분야에서 성공할 수 있습니다. 당신의 개 볼 수 있나요? 이게 첫 번째입니다. 그녀의 이름은 모모입니다. 이것은 제 열 달 된 웰시 코기입니다. 그녀, 그녀의 이름은 레나 멜로디입니다. 좋아요. 이 아이는 헤르미온느이고 펫스마트 안의 상자에 버려졌어요. 너무 귀엽고, 너무 소중해요. 에일린, 오늘 이야기를 나눠주셔서 정말 감사합니다. 정말 즐거웠어요. 네, 감사합니다. 저는 케이틀린 오늘은 수의사 테크니션 에일린과 함께한 글레이드오 온 더 고 편이었습니다. 다음에 또 뵙겠습니다.

title: Meet Ritika - Registered Nurse
transcript: ﻿1 This video is presented to you by Gladeo on the Go and the Los Angeles Opportunity Youth Collaborative. A network of multi-sector partnerships working together to better connect foster youth with education, jobs, and opportunity so they can flourish and thrive. Hi, I'm Katelyn and today we get to meet Ritika. Hi, my name is Ritika. I'm a registered nurse. I just moved to New York and I got my New York license about four months ago. I worked in the MICU, which stands for Medical Intensive Care Unit over at Cleveland Clinic and now I pivoted, so I wanted move states and I always wanna live in New York. So moved to New York and I'll be pivoting from working with adults to kids. And what do you love most about your job? What gets you excited to go to work each day? I like learning a lot and I like learning about diseases themself and you know, just like this is something down the pathophysiology, why comes by something comes to be. I like taking care of people, I like providing them with care and then just like helping them in whatever way I can and knowing that I did the best I could when I walk out of my shift. Was there something or someone that inspired this career track for you or how did you make that decision or why did you make that decision? I was born in India and I came here when I was like five or six years old. I was here for like two years, went back in 2001 because my dad was really sick and he needed like extra support. And then eventually my grandma, she like had fractured her leg and she was bedridden for about three months. We had to take care of her. I remember like helping her like walk. I was like, well grandma, you can do it. I'm like, you know, like little nine or 10-year-old me like telling her you can walk around 'cause you know, she was like my best friend at the time. We would always do everything together and I always knew I wanted to be a nurse in the back of my mind just because I had to get my stepdad, I mean my dad, my grandma, and my mother eventually ended up becoming a nurse, too. But yeah, like I went out to nursing school and I think that was the best like couple years of my life I have. That I, where I pushed myself where I always told myself like no matter what happens, you get this career, your career will never leave. Your career will always be around, you'll always be a successful woman with this. I'd love to hear a little bit more about your backstory and maybe some of the things that have led you to who you are and where you are today just so we can get to know you a little bit more. Like my senior of high school, I ended up going into foster care, I was a foster care child. I didn't like to talk about it that I was in foster care with people 'cause there was a stigma around it and I just didn't want like to know who I was, where I was coming from, you know. Then I went, went up to undergrad, got my degree in neuroscience, which was so hard. Ugh. I, the time was so hard because I just didn't know how to study and I struggled a lot, and I struggled with like maintaining relationships or maintaining like even friendships, struggled with like my classes a lot and I just didn't not ask for, ask for help because I didn't wanna be vulnerable. I didn't wanna be like, oh, she's not gonna come sooner. She doesn't understand things and I wish I did not have that in my mind. I wish I was able to easily ask like, Hey, I need some help. My undergrad GP was not the greatest. It was very bad. I was not the smartest student. I think I just felt like when I was an undergrad doing neuroscience, even though I was doing it, I just didn't know how to study. My brain works in a way that it's slow. I don't get things fast. I'm not a person like, oh, read something I'll understand in power. No, I need to understand the things from the bottom. I got into a top nursing school in the country. I was freaking shocked and I was like, what the hell? And I became a nurse officially when I turned, when I was 27. If you could offer some advice to your younger self and the young people watching this right now, what would you say? Don't be afraid of asking questions. Like seriously don't be. If I can go back in that like 10 years ago and tell myself that, I would say, if you are confused about something and you're sitting in a big, like 1000 people lecture, ask a d*** question. It doesn't matter if someone, if you're, if you're afraid, somebody else in the class is afraid too. And you have to find your group of people that are gonna be help you grow and thrive. And it's okay if you're focused on just on yourself for the time being like, that's okay, it is okay because once you focus on yourself, you thrive, you're succeeding, you'll be able to help other people. If you can't even help yourself, you cannot help somebody else. Like, and that, that's what I'm telling you. It's okay. Me going up to nursing school, leaving freaking California, going up to Maryland in the east coast. I know like I message some relationships. My family, my friends, they wanted me to stick around and I didn't. I chose something to do for myself and now like I went over, I'm like, I went over and I thrive. Yeah, but I, I feel like I just like said a lot and it's okay because that's what some people's minds work and that's how my my ADHD mind works as well. I have it and did not know I even had it, but I realize when I'm talking, I'm doing certain things, I'm like, okay, definitely comes down. So. Ritika, thank you so much for taking the time to do this today. It's been so wonderful getting to meet you and getting to know you a little bit. Thank you. Thank you. I'm Katelyn and this is Gladeo in partnership with OYC. See ya.

transcript: 嗨， 我是 Katelyn ，这是与 Create California 合作的 Gladeo on the Go Zoom 版。0 今天我们有幸采访到一位教学艺术家 嗨， 我 名字是 Miko Lee，我是 教学艺术家 教学艺术家是双职业人士。0 专业艺术家又 教育家 因此，专业艺术家可以是 你能想象到的艺术形式。诗人、 戏剧演员、音乐、 舞蹈、视觉艺术。对我来说 是戏剧。0 晚上我会 旧金山， 观看莎士比亚的表演。0 白天我在斯托克顿青年管理局 与年轻的囚犯一起做戏剧和多媒体工作 所以我同时做这两 ， 互相启发，帮助我成长为一名艺术家 和一个人。0 你最喜欢这份工作的 ？ 为什么这对你来说如此充实？ 我喜欢教授艺术，因为它 教别人画画或 别人表演 独白。0 这些都很棒， 这些 很重要， 但真正的意义在于找到你是 的根源，你如何融入这个世界， 你想说些什么才能成为我们生活的 世界的积极参与者 我们现在生活在 走出 流行病， 包括 COVID-19 和随后在我们国家酝酿已久的种族 。0 这一切都如此复杂、痛苦 真实。0 然而 我们追根溯源时， 我们都是人。 我们都有故事可讲，而我们相互学习的方式 讲述我们的故事并分享我们是谁。 人们进入这个行业的方式可能有很多， 但您独特的道路是什么？ 您是如何进入这个行业的？ 我认为对于许多教学艺术家来说， 我们以非常不同的方式进入这个领域。 我们大多数人都想成为专业艺术家， 因此我们总是忙于获得尽可能多的工作。 我二十出头的时候，刚从大学毕业， 就旅行了一年 参加了戏剧《贞子与千鹤》。 当我们巡演时，主要是学校 和社区中心， 他们要求我们谈论这件事。 所以我认为那就是开始，非常温和地 只是在戏剧表演后引导问答环节 之后是一周的研讨会， 然后是教学， 之后我被邀请到越来越多的地方教授它。0 我越来越喜欢它，因为它以 与 专业艺术截然不同的方式激励着我 您有什么建议，或者您希望在您的旅程中了解 可以传承给下一代的事情 你想成为一名教学艺术家 的建议是找一位导师。找 能够完全震撼你的世界的人 让你以不同的方式思考世界， 挑战你 让你能够每天提升自己的水平。0 这个人不一定是你所在艺术领域的 个人。0 可以是， 如果你是一个陶艺家，0 可以找到 比任何人都更了解乐 的 ， 你知道 能让你继续前进， 那就太好了。 也可以是祖父母， 是孩子， 是某个挑战你，让你换位思考的人。因为在教授艺术方面， 正是我们努力的方向。不断挑战自己， 要 我们的艺术形式，更 我们如何看待自己反映世界 以及我们想要为整个世界做出的贡献 你身边没有这样的人， 联系我们的教学艺术家协会 我们有这张资产地图，实际上是 完整的地图 是一张世界地图。教学艺术家把自己 那里， 自己的艺术形式 自己是谁，你可以查找这个人，建立联系。实际上，正是通过这种合作 我们在合作中成长 在创作中成长。 就是我们的全部意义所在。 谢谢你的建议，Miko，非常感谢你今天和我们分享你的故事 你真是太棒 。0 谢谢 邀请。0 我 Katelyn，这是 Gladeo on the Go Zoom 版，与 California 合作。0 下次见。

title: Real-Life Story: Holly, Welder
transcript: ﻿1 Top Skills Needed. Workers in these fields require focus, attention to detail, and a strong work ethic. Other skills needed include: Business Acumen. Many positions require business acumen to understand how to design and manufacture a product cost effectively, then sell it at a profit. Communication Skills. Communicating ideas to others is essential, such as explaining designs to engineers, manufacturers, and consumers. Creativity. Whether they're improving existing products or designing new ones, workers must be creative, curious, and imaginative. Flexibility. Any issue that causes production delays is expensive, so workers must be flexible and ready to adapt to changing circumstances while continuing to make progress. Problem-Solving. Problems can come up when making any product, so it's important to anticipate, identify, and resolve issues in a timely fashion. Teamwork Skills. Most roles in this sector serve as part of a larger, often interdisciplinary team that has to get along and get things done on time! Technical Skills. Workers commonly rely on software for computer aided design in manufacturing, simulations, visualizations, 3D printing, robotics, and automation.

title: Real-Life Story: Marnie, Financial Manager
transcript: ﻿1 제 이름은 마니입니다. 저는 Fox Searchlight Pictures의 재무 및 운영 부사장입니다. Fox Searchlight Pictures는 독립 영화를 제작 및 인수합니다. 내가 프로듀스라고 하면 은 우리가 실제로 영화를 자체적으로 만들고 인수하면 선댄스 같은 영화제에 가고 완성된 영화를 얻는다는 뜻입니다. 과 은 우리가 제작하고 획득하는 모든 필름에 대해 을 전 세계에 판매하고 배포합니다. Fox Searchlight가 한 영화는 Slumdog Millionaire, Juno 및 Black Swan입니다. 내 책임 중 일부는 1, 나는 연간 예산 책정 과정을 감독합니다. 그것은 우리가 년과 2년 동안 벌고 지출할 것으로 예상되는 돈을 으로 추정한다는 것을 의미합니다. 우리가 하는 돈을 모두 관리하고 개선합니다 영화 예고편, 및 홍보 투어와 같은 일에 . 간단히 말해서 나는 우리 회사가 현명한 재무 결정을 내릴 수 있도록 돕습니다. 내가 가장 좋아하는 것은 창조 산업에서 일하는 동안 재무 두뇌 를 사용할 수 있다는 것입니다. 20세기 학교 재학 중에 인턴십을 했습니다. Fox Film 그리고 그 인턴쉽을 하는 동안 최선을 다해 일했고 기대 이상 결실을 맺었습니다 졸업했을 때 수석 재무 분석가로서 초급 직업을 얻을 수 있었기 때문입니다. 나의 마지막 충고는 당신의 경력 경로가 항상 직선이 수도 있기 때문에 열린 마음을 유지해야 한다는 것입니다. 그러나 핵심은 열심히 일하고 호기심을 유지하는 것입니다.

title: Real-Life Story: Taj, Venture Capitalist
transcript: 저는 케이틀린이고 오늘은 로스앤젤레스 클린테크 인큐베이터에 와서 타지와 만나 가장 빠르게 성장하는 산업 중 하나인 클린테크에 대해 이야기해 보겠습니다. 시작해 볼까요. 타지. 안녕하세요. 오늘 함께해 주셔서 정말 감사합니다. 감사합니다. 안녕하세요 케이틀린. 잘 지내세요? 좋아요, 잘 지내세요? 좋아요, 좋아요. 멋지네요. 벤처 캐피털리즘은 많은 사람들이 충분히 알지 못하는 것 같아요. 그렇죠. 하지만 알아야 해요. 멋지고, 정말 중요하거든요. 그렇죠 그럼, 타지, 시작하기에 앞서 벤처 캐피털리즘이 무엇인지 말씀해 주시겠어요? 그렇죠. 그래서 벤처 캐피털리즘에 대해 생각할 때, 스테로이드를 맞은 은행 같은 느낌이 들어요. 아시다시피, 많은 사람들이 은행에 가서 사업을 시작할 돈을 구합니다. 사람들은 사업을 시작할 돈을 얻기 위해 벤처 캐피탈리스트에게 옵니다. 사업을 키우고 싶어하는 사람들도 있습니다. 차이점은 우리는 다른 사람의 돈을 관리한다는 것입니다. 그래서 우리는 업 사업에 투자하고 싶어하는 다른 사람들의 돈을 받아서 돈을 투자할 만한 훌륭한 회사를 . 이에 더하여, 저는 엔젤 투자자 이고 클린테크가 아니지만 영향을 미치는 회사에 투자합니다. 0 엔젤 투자자. 그게 뭐죠? VC 다른 개인의 돈을 관리하므로 부유한 사람들을 데려갑니다. 르브론 제임스가 회사에 돈을 주면 회사는 르브론의 돈을 그와 무수히 많은 것에 투자할 수 있다고 생각해 보세요. 투자자는 자신의 돈을 투자합니다 그리고 제가 돈을 모은 방법은 제가 초기 스타트업 회사의 직원 과 근로자였습니다.그들은 인수되었고 회사가 인수되면 더 큰 회사가 때때로 그 금액으로 그들을 사들입니다.그리고 저는 그 돈을 가지고 제가 믿는 다른 회사와 다른 창업자들에게 투자했습니다 그래서 정말 좋은 기회이지만 듣는 것도 정말 흥미롭습니다.0 로스앤젤레스 테크 인큐베이터, 혹은 여러분이 부르는 LACI의 역사에 대해 조금 말씀해 주시겠습니까 민간 자본의 지원을 받기 때문에 정말 흥미롭습니다.0 심지어 우리가 이 건물 및 전력부에서 한 것입니다. 그래서 LACI는 기업의 미래와 클린테크를 진정으로 발전시키기 위해 그러한 단체에 의해 개발되었습니다. 그래서 당신은 VC에서 일하고 많은 스타트업 대회의 심사위원이기도 했습니다. 네. 네. 그럼 당신과 당신의 회사가 아이디어에 투자하는 것을 고려할 때 을 찾는다고 생각하십니까? 글쎄요, 저는 인큐베이터에서 일하는데, 스타트업을 위한 학교라고 생각하시면 됩니다. 알겠죠? 그곳은 기업이 하는 일을 이해하고 배우는 방법을 제공하고 더 나은 회사가 되기 위한 도구를 얻는 데 도움이 됩니다. 그래서 우리는 에너지 스타트업에 집중하고 재생 에너지나 태양열과 관련된 모든 집중합니다. 또한 테슬라와 같은 운송 수단에 집중하고 순환 경제라고 하는 것에도 집중합니다. 0 순환 경제는 제품을 다시 사용할 수 있도록 재활용하는 것입니다. 우유통을 가져다가 그 우유통으로 스웨터를 만드는 회사가 있는데, 정말 멋지지 않나요? 그렇죠. 낭비를 줄이는 동시에 제품에 두 번째 을 불어넣기도 합니다. 우리는 일반적으로 그런 분야에 있는 회사를 찾으려고 노력합니다. 이 조직인 에 대해 제가 좋아하는 또 다른 점은 LACI가 다양성에 중점을 둔다는 것입니다. 그래서 우리는 여성이나 종인 창업자와 그런 분야를 찾으려고 노력합니다. 그래서 그들이 속한 산업을 조사하고, 팀이 훌륭한지 보고, 그런 다음 그에 따라 투자를 합니다. 제가 하는 일을 철저히 즐기는 사람처럼 보였습니다. 네. 네. 제가 즐기는 것은 무언가가 만들어지는 것을 보는 것입니다. Uber, Twitter, TikTok 같은 것에 대해 생각할 때, 누군가를 위한 아이디어였고, 그것을 현실 만들기 위해서는 자본과 노력이 필요했고, 저는 그것을 좋아합니다. 그래서 우리는 조금 전에 당신의 여정이 약간 특이한 것에 대해 이야기했습니다. 네. 그래서 당신은 시와 문학을 전공했습니다. 저는 래퍼가 되고 싶었습니다. 네. 저는 80년대에 자랐고 힙합과 음악이 주변에 있었습니다. 고등학교 때 글쓰기와 문학에 정말 능숙했고 저는 아, 달라질 수 있겠다'고 생각했습니다. 그래서 제가 어렸을 때 생각했던 궤적이었습니다. 네. 그래서 저는 경제학 MBA와 경제학 학위를 가지고 있습니다. 영어: 지금까지 시와 문학에서 멀리 떨어져 있지만, 우리가 다루는 모든 것은 스토리텔링에 관한 것입니다. 누군가 자신이 지금 있는 곳에서 끝나고 싶다는 것을 일찍부터 알고 있습니다. 네. 대학 전공자들에게 고려해야 할 제안이 있습니까? 다양한 전공이 들어오기 때문에 열려 있다고 생각합니다. 호기심이 있어야 한다고 생각합니다. 문학 이든, 건강 이든, 금융 전공이든 그게 중요한 부분이라고 생각합니다. 자연스럽게 호기심이 많은 것은 0 이 직업에 종사하는 데 필요한 전제 조건과 같습니다. 많이 읽을 것이기 때문에 전혀 모르는 좋습니다. 어렸을 때, 어땠어요? 당신에게 이 길을 예고한 것이 있었나요? 글쎄요, 어머니께서 말씀하셨죠. 저는 위키피디아가 나오기 전에는 항상 책을 읽었어요. 백과사전이나 딱딱한 책이라는 작은 책이 있었는데, 사람들이 집집마다 다니며 팔곤 했어요. 저희 어머니가 그걸 사서 구독하면 매주 한 책을 주셨어요. 0 저는 언어에 매료되었고, 단어와 단어가 어떻게 그림을 그릴 수 있는지에 매료되었어요. 저는 매우, 시각적인 사람이었어요. 그럼 지금은 어때요, 직장 외에 하고 싶은 게 있나요? 저는 음악에 정말 관심이 많아요. 제 아내는 가수이고, 그녀는 실내악 가수예요. 제 아들은 음악가라서 아들이 연주하는 걸 듣는 걸 좋아해요. 하지만 저는 음악 산업을 보고, 제가 하는 일과 비교했어요. 농담이었어요. 모두에게 주제가가 있고, 저는 그쪽 일한다고요. 당신을 보고 언젠가 여기 오길 바라는 사람에게 조언이 있나요? 벤처 캐피탈 와 엔젤 투자자들은 항상 파괴적인 기업을 찾습니다. 벤처 캐피탈리스트 자체도 파괴적일 겁니다. 가장 좋은 자질 중 하나는 호기심이 많고, 항상 공부하고, 항상 읽는 것이라고 생각합니다. 그리고 아시다시피, 세상은 이 산업에서 그들의 것이 될 수 있습니다.

title: Real-Life Story: Wendy, Digital Marketer
transcript: 안녕하세요, 저는 Katelyn 이고, 여기는 Gladeo on the Go Zoom 에디션입니다. 오늘은 Foothill College의 디지털 마케팅 자격증 프로그램을 수료하고 현재 디지털 마케팅 분야에서 일하는 Wendy와 이야기해 보겠습니다. 제 이름은 Wendy입니다. 저는 최근 Foothill College에서 디지털 마케팅 자격증을 받았고, 지금은 Frequence 스타트업에서 디지털 마케팅 운영 분석가로 일하고 있습니다. 디지털 마케팅이란 일까요? 네, 디지털 마케팅은 온라인 마케팅 거의 비슷합니다. 즉, 인터넷과 다른 디지털 커뮤니케이션 을 사용하여 브랜드를 잠재 고객과 연결하도록 홍보하는 것과 같습니다. 저희 팀은 소셜 미디어를 전문으로 . Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, Twitter와 같은 소셜 미디어 을 사용합니다. Frequence 의 일반적인 하루는 어떻게 되나요? 그래서 Frequence에서의 제 하루는, 다시피, 매일 아침 회의를 합니다. 모든 운영팀이 모여서 마케팅 캠페인의 양과 0 각 티켓을 누가 담당할지에 대해 이야기합니다. 또한 하루에 얼마나 많은 업데이트가 있는지, 예산 업데이트, 항공편, 항공편 업데이트, 기타 또는 크리에이티브 업데이트가 확인합니다. 그녀의 게임 플랜을 살펴본 후, 실제로 조치를 취하고 을 모니터링하여 최대한의 잠재력을 발휘하여 노출을 제공하고 있는지 확인합니다. 마케팅에 관심을 갖게 된 계기는 무엇입니까? 글쎄요, 기술은 지난 몇 년 동안 발전했고, 기업들이 어떻게 사업을 확장할 수 있는지 정말 흥미롭게 생각합니다. 훨씬 더 넓은 대상 고객에게 다가가 이를 통해 더 많은 수익을 창출할 수 있습니다. 광고는 아시다시피 의사소통의 한 방법이고 저는 회사들이 고객에게 자사 제품에 대해 의사소통하기 위해 다양한 전략을 어떻게 내놓는지 항상 정말 흥미롭게 생각했습니다. 당신의 직업에서 가장 좋은 점은 무엇입니까? 제가 제 직업에서 가장 좋아하는 점은 끊임없이 도전을 받고 거의 매일 새로운 것을 배운다는 것입니다. 아시다시피 마케팅 분석, 마케팅 캠페인 분석, 성과를 확인하기 위한 데이터부터 0 Facebook에서 구현하는 새로운 광고 정책까지 네, 그게 제 직업에서 가장 좋아하는 점입니다. 끊임없이 배우고 있다는 것입니다. Foothill College의 디지털 프로그램이 이 분야에서 성공하는 데 어떻게 준비시켜 주었다고 생각하십니까? 디지털 마케팅 자격증은 광고 운영 업계에 진출하는 데 필요한 기술 제공했습니다. 그들은 모든 것을 실제로 상호 작용적으로 만드는 디지털 학습 플랫폼 을 제공하고 실제 세계에 적용하는 데 필요한 교육과 도구 제공합니다. Pathstream 매우 사용자 친화적이고 상호 작용적이기 때문에 실제 세계를 연습할 수 있는 훌륭한 도구입니다. 그렇다면 업계에서 첫 직업을 어떻게 얻었습니까? 그래서 저는 제안을 받기 전에 많은 거절을 받았습니다 저는 조금 낙담했습니다. 아시다시피, 많은 회사와 면접을 보았고 면접 과정을 거쳤고 그 중 일부는 대부분 저에게 전화를 돌려주지 않았지만 하지만 제가 가장 예상하지 못했을 때 저는 두 개의 다른 회사에서 하나의 채용 제안이 아니라 두 개의 다른 채용 제안을 받았습니다. 결국 저는 회사를 거절했고 저는 제 가치에 잘 맞는 회사를 선택했습니다. 당신이 결코 포기하지 않아서 지금 있는 것이 좋습니다. 여정에서 직면했던 다른 장벽이 있었나요? 저는 4명의 형제 자매 중 세대 졸업생입니다. 그래서 저는 항상 직업 목표를 달성하고 인생에서 성공해야 한다고 생각했습니다. 제가 직면 장벽 중 하나는 언어였습니다. 영어 제 모국어가 아니라 제2 언어이기 때문입니다. 그래서 때때로 자신감이 부족하다고 느꼈습니다 제 경우에는 모국어가 영어이고 제 경우에는 스페인어 아닌 다른 사람 선택할 것이라고 생각했기 때문입니다. 어떻게 그걸 극복하셨나요? 헤쳐나갔나요? 네, 제게 많은 도움이 된 것은 우선 연습이었습니다. 아시다시피, 인터뷰를 준비 방법에 대한 YouTube 동영상을 많이 본 것 . 그들은, 그들은 연습할 수 있는 정말 좋은 질문 세트를 가지고 있고 또한, 방법에 대한 팁을 주는 사람들이 많이 있습니다. 고용주에게 질문해야 질문의 유형이나 옷 입는 방법 같은 것들 말입니다. 저는 YouTube 동영상이 정말 도움이 된다고 생각합니다. 그들은 여러분이 실행할 수 있는 좋은 팁을 많이 가지고 있습니다. 훌륭합니다. 좋은 조언입니다. 그럼 직장 외에, 취미와 관심사는 입니까? 네, 제 취미 중 하나는 미니 오시 강아지와 시간 보내는 것입니다. 그의 이름은 핀입니다. 그리고 네, 저는 그냥, 아시다시피, 활동적인 것, 산책, 운동을 하고, 그리고 믿거나 말거나, 아시다시피, 저는 광고를 보는 것을 좋아하는데, 그런 것들은 회사들이 내놓는 것입니다. 고객을 유치하기 위해 좋은 전략을 사용하고 있는지 여부 평가하는 것은 흥미로운 일입니다. 보여드릴까요? 네, 부탁드립니다. 디지털 마케팅에 관심이 있는 사람들에게 조언이 있으신 ? 네, 제 조언은 낙담하지 말라는 것입니다 첫 번째 시도에서 일자리를 얻지 못할 것입니다. 아시다시피 첫 직장을 얻기 전에 많은 회사와 면접을 0 보아야 합니다. 그래서 많은 노력과 일관성이 필요하지만 결국에는 그곳에 도달할 것입니다. 웬디, 디지털 마케팅 분야에서 경력, 어떤 것인지, 어떻게 그곳에 도달했는지에 대해 이야기해 주셔서 감사합니다. 정말 즐거웠습니다. 네, 감사합니다. 저는 케이틀린이고, 여기는 Gladeo on the Go Zoom 에디션입니다. 다음에 봐요.

transcript: ﻿1 Industry Trends. The Education and Training sector is evolving with technology being increasingly incorporated into the classroom. In addition, remote learning has become more of an option for students who face barriers to access or just need some extra flexibility. Another trend is that STEM programs are recognizing the value of creativity and artistic elements with many schools using the term STEAM to include the letter A for arts. Meanwhile, there's a push in early childhood education to provide more high-quality education earlier in life in order to boost the odds of academic and career success later! title: Meet Chrissi - Creative Director
transcript: Hello, my name is Chrissi Hernandez. I'm a creative director in Los Angeles, California. A creative director can mean quite a few things in different industries. For me personally, it means a person who comes up with the ideas for photoshoots and campaigns for websites. And in my case, that would be mostly fashion and beauty websites. But when I was at Sephora, I had this great mentor named Mia and she said to me once, treat every project like it's something that you would want to put in your portfolio. And that gave me a really different perspective on how I view my work and how I felt at work because if I come in every day and I really want to be proud of what I'm doing, then I've got to put all my energy and effort and like give a little bit of myself to it rather than just thinking of it as a job. When I was growing up, I always really loved drawing like my mom and my grandma would always go to this cute little coffee shop, and I remember we'd meet up there like two or three times a week and every time we'd go, I ordered fries and my mom would give me a napkin and a pen and I would just draw for hours. Growing up, you know, my dad had his own upholstery shop, so he was, in his own way, very creative. He would, you know, and I remember like, when I was a kid he would let me and my brother like do the button machine or like he would teach us how to sew little pouches for pencils and things like that. When I was in high school, I had this really great teacher who brought people in from different colleges and universities or different professionals that would actually talk to us about all the things you can do as a creative career. And that was really an eye-opener for me because I was the first in my family to go to college and so there weren't a lot of people that I could talk to and communicate with about what options were out there. Through a series of events, I ended up with a job interview at Fox which I was very excited and nervous about. I ended up getting the job and it was a contract job which means, you know, it's only for it can be ended at any time. It's hourly job. I only ended up lasting at that job for four months because I was so inexperienced. And that was that was tough. because, you know, I was, I was so excited. I had this big break and then immediately like, it had fallen out of my hands. And then I had another job that I learned catalogue design and learning how to do logo design and hang tags. So then, I was building more skills. So I think with each failure, came an opportunity to grow and learn and so, you know, definitely I got down sometimes but what I I realized after a period of time is that it actually made me a stronger designer at the end. So sometimes in failures, you can find really amazing successes. Besides work, I pretty much love doing everything you can possibly think of... I am an avid adventurer, traveler. I've now been to over 46 countries. I love hiking, rock climbing, whitewater rafting, just about anything. Just about anything really, anything active. I love to do. But then I also love traveling because you get introduced to so many different types of cultures and people and see all kinds of different architecture. So, I just love absorbing all of those things in life.

title: Real Life Story: Dr. Diane, Professor of Plant Sciences
transcript: ﻿1 Industry Trends. The occupations in this sector are affected by ever-evolving industry trends, changing climate conditions, and updated policies and priorities. For instance, agricultural workers are always developing ways to make crops more resilient, to withstand changing temperatures and rainfall amounts. Workers also look for ways to reduce pollution and mitigate the effects of deforestation to protect resources for future generations. Trends in food production are focused on sustainability, automation, and personalized nutrition to meet consumer demands. Natural resources trends are also shifting towards sustainable practices, technological innovation, and circular economy models to meet growing global demands. Supply chain problems have disrupted the process of getting food from farms to tables impacting availability and prices. Meanwhile, technology like drones and sensors are helping to make processes more efficient. These are just a few of the trends happening in these dynamic fields. The Agricultural, Food, and Natural Resources sector is crucial to our society. Without it, we wouldn't have most of the foods we eat, fibers for the clothes we wear, or renewable energy sources we use every day. Curious about which pathways might match your personality and interest in this sector? Then let's take the Sector Quiz!

transcript: , soy Katelyn y este es Gladeo on the Go edición en asociación con Foothill College. estoy hablando con Heather. es exalumna de Foothill y ahora trabaja como higienista dental. , llamo Heather Izquierdo. higienista dental registrada. higienista registrado es alguien que limpia dientes de los pacientes y les enseña cuidar mejor sus dientes. Cuál es tu favorita de este trabajo? es la relación que con los pacientes. decir, conoces muchos tipos diferentes de personas, crecer a sus hijos. Creo que higiene dental es la carrera perfecta para equilibrio entre el trabajo y la familia. trabajar tan poco como un día a la semana y seis o siete días a la semana. ¿Puedes hablar un poco sobre experiencia en Foothill? En realidad, creo que es un requisito como una de las clases, y definitivamente encuentra un buen compañero de estudio porque vas a estudiar mucho. ¿Qué pasa fuera del trabajo? title: Meet Mikaela, Pharmacy Technician
transcript: 안녕하세요, 저는 케이틀린입니다 여기는 Gladeo on the Go Zoom 버전입니다. 오늘은 Foothill College 졸업생이자 수의사 기술자인 Aileen을 만나보겠습니다. 그녀의 이야기를 들어보시죠. 안녕하세요, 저는 Aileen입니다. 저는 수의사 기술자입니다. 수의사 기술자로서 무슨 일을 하시나요? RN, 등록된 병원 간호사라고 생각해 보세요. 엑스레이, 실험실 작업, 예방 접종, 0 치과 선택적 시술 중성화 수술, 필요한 경우 절단, 필요한 경우 대량 제거를 합니다. 또한 애완동물의 여행 건강 증명서도 발급합니다. 여행을 하려면 법적으로 올바른 서류가 있어야 하는데 사람뿐만 아니라 애완동물에게도 필요합니다. 졸업 후 어떻게 이 직업을 꽤 빨리 얻게 되셨나요? 저희는 정말 수요가 많습니다. 안타깝게도 저희 같은 사람은 많지 않아요. 그래서, 아시다시피, RVT가 일자리를 찾고 있을 때, 모든 병원에서 "저를 낚아채고 싶어요."라고 말하죠. 당신은 이런 모든 경험과 지식을 가지고 있고, 병원에 있어서 큰 자산이 될 거라고 생각해요. 하지만 지금 직장에서는 일종의 도약을 했습니다 아시다시피, 저는 학교를 졸업했고, 일반 진료에서 모든 경험을 가지고 있습니다. 네, 응급이나 전문 분야는 아니지만, 제가 무엇을 할 수 있는지 알 만큼은 충분히 있습니다. 거의 즉시 채용되었습니다. 그들은, 아시다시피, 물론, 시간 노력을 그 분야에 대한 동기 부여와 열정이 있는 사람에게 투자하고 싶어했습니다. 그러니까 당신은 공식적으로 수의사 기술자군요. 항상 이것이 당신이 하고 싶은 일이라는 걸 알고 있었나요? 항상 수의사 기술자가 되고 싶었던 아니었어요. 어렸을 때 동물을 좋아해서 항상 주변에 동물들이 있었지만 그게 제가 하고 싶은 일이라는 걸 전혀 몰랐어요. 정말 친한 친구가 연락을 해왔어요. 야, 내가 수의사 기술자가 될 거야. 라고 했고, 저는 대체 그게 뭐지?라고 생각했죠 그래서 친구가 그 이야기를 해줬고, 저는 와, 정말 흥미로운 ."라고 생각했어요. 친구가 설명해 줬어요. 수의사 기술자는 환자와 더 많은 시간을 보내야 한다고요 수의사 기술자로서 환자와 많은 시간을 보내야 하는데, 저는 오, 정말 좋다."라고 생각했어요 이 직업에서 가장 좋아하는 부분은 무엇인가요? 지금 제 직업에서 가장 좋아하는 부분은 출근해서 팀이 준비된 모습을 보는 것입니다. 저를 든든하게 지지해 주는 팀이 있다는 를 뒷받침해 줄 뿐만 아니라 자신이 하는 일에 대한 열정과 사랑을 느낄 수 있기 때문입니다. 정말 큰 차이를 만듭니다. Foothill College에서의 경험은 어땠나요? Foothill 정말 훌륭했습니다. 제가 만났던 선생님들은 정말 배려심이 많았습니다. 미안합니다. 그들이 그리워요. 눈물이 나려고 합니다. 프로그램 디렉터부터 파트타임 직원 모든 선생님들이 정말 훌륭합니다. 학교에 없을 때도 질문이 있는 경우 이메일을 보내면 쉬는 날에 답변해 줍니다. 당신은 1세대 대학생이고, 그건 엄청난 성취입니다. 당신에게는 무슨 의미가 있나요? 저에게는, 엄마 그녀의 가족 베트남 전쟁에서 도망쳤다는 이유만으로 엄청난 압박감을 느꼈습니다. 그들은 배를 타고 여기 와서 생계 유지하기 위해 애썼고, 자녀들에게 최고의 삶을 주기 위해 애썼습니다. 그래서 저는 세대 대학생으로서, 엄마를 스럽게 만들고 싶었고, 엄마의 모든 노고가 헛되지 않았다는 것을 해주고 싶었습니다. 가족 중에서 처음으로 대학에 진학하면서 어려움은 있었나요 네, 그저 제가 원하는 곳에 도달하기 위해 어떤 단계를 거쳐야 하는지 알아내는 과정일 뿐이었습니다 제 가족은 대학에 가고, 취직을 한다고 했기 때문에 정말 힘들었습니다. 그럼 그 사이의 단계는 뭐죠? 그들은, 모르겠어요, 알아내라고 했습니다. 내 생각은, 오, 좋네. 수의사 기술자로 적합한 사람을 설명해 줄 수 있나요? 배우고자 하는 의지가 있는 사람, 학교에서 배우는 것 같지는 않지만, 직장에서, 직장 밖에서 계속해서 배우는 사람. 그들은 큰 마음을 가져야 합니다. 그들은 깨어나 인 태도를 갖도록 동기를 부여받고, 인내심을 갖고, 자신 자비를 베풀어야 합니다. 물론 사람들과 그들의 애완동물에게도요. 또한 훌륭한 리더십도 필요합니다. 아시다시피, 그들은 무언가 잘못되었을 때 목소리를 냅니다. 일을 주도적으로 합니다. 누구나 수의사 기술자가 될 수 있습니다. 그런 특성 있다면 이 분야에서 성공할 수 있습니다. 당신의 개 볼 수 있나요? 이게 첫 번째입니다. 그녀의 이름은 모모입니다. 이것은 제 열 달 된 웰시 코기입니다. 그녀, 그녀의 이름은 레나 멜로디입니다. 좋아요. 이 아이는 헤르미온느이고 펫스마트 안의 상자에 버려졌어요. 너무 귀엽고, 너무 소중해요. 에일린, 오늘 이야기를 나눠주셔서 정말 감사합니다. 정말 즐거웠어요. 네, 감사합니다. 저는 케이틀린 오늘은 수의사 테크니션 에일린과 함께한 글레이드오 온 더 고 편이었습니다. 다음에 또 뵙겠습니다.

title: Meet Greisy, Program Director and Artist
transcript: WEBVTT - https://maestrasuite.com ¿Ves, eh, que tu identidad como artista se refleja de alguna manera en esta carrera? Como... Mil por ciento. No estaría en el aula si no fuera artista. Cuando comencé a asumir este rol, me sentí realmente como si tuviera mucho síndrome del impostor y me pregunté: ¿soy realmente la persona adecuada? Es decir, no tengo formación académica. En cierto modo, me llevo bien con los jóvenes, y yo era el joven que necesitaba desarrollarse a esa edad. Me comuniqué con mi profesor de inglés de la escuela secundaria y le dije: "Oye, estoy estresado. No estoy segura de ser la persona adecuada." Y ella dice: "Hiciste teatro. Un profesor es un actor frustrado." Y yo digo, "Vaya, ¿qué quieres decir?" Y la verdad es que estoy al frente del aula intentando asegurarme de que alguien se mantenga conectado conmigo durante 45 minutos sin salir del auditorio ni del aula, y tengo que ser atractivo, carismático, divertido. Suficiente para que les importe lo que estoy diciendo. La otra parte del éxito de mi viaje ha sido saber exactamente lo que hago bien en una cosa y cómo esa cosa se transfiere a otra. Entonces, en serio, estoy... Soy anfitrión de micrófono abierto. Esa habilidad de compromiso me ayuda en el aula frente a los estudiantes. El nivel de intencionalidad e intensidad que necesitas tener al hacer películas es el mismo tipo de intensidad y enfoque que necesito si estoy creando infraestructura dentro de una organización o si estoy organizando un evento en vivo para jóvenes. Cuando tengas una experiencia, sácala a la luz. ¿Qué aprendí aquí? ¿A dónde me puede llevar esto?

title: Meet Aileen, Vet Tech
transcript: Hi, I'm Katelyn And this is Gladeo on the Go, Zoom edition. Today we get to meet Aileen a vet tech and Foothill college graduate. Let's hear her story. Hi, my name is Aileen. I'm a veterinary technician. So what do you do as a vet tech? Think of it as like your RN, your registered nurses at your hospitals. We do from x-rays, lab work, vaccines, dental, elective procedures like spay and neuters, amputations, if needed. Mass removals, if it's needed. We also do travel health certificates for pets. They legally need to have the correct documentation to be able to travel not just people, but pets need it too. How did you get your foot in the door to get this job pretty quickly after graduation? We definitely are in very high demand. Unfortunately, there's not a lot of us out there. So when, you know, there's an RVT looking for a job, all the hospitals are like I want to snatch you up. You have all this experience and knowledge. Like it's a great asset for a hospital to have. But at my current job, I kind of took the leap of, you know, I'm done with school and I have all these experiences with general practices. Yes it's not emergency or specialty but I have enough where I know what I can do. I got hired almost immediately. They wanted to, you know, of course, put time and effort into someone that is motivated and passionate about the field. So you're a vet tech officially, did you always know that this was what you wanted to do? I did not always know I wanted to be a vet tech. When I was younger, I had a love for animals. So I've always had animals around me, but I never knew. that's what I wanted to do. A really good friend of mine he reached out to me and he was like, hey like I'm gonna be a vet tech and I'm like, what on Earth is that? So he told me about it and I was like, wow, that sounds so interesting. So he explains to me, you got to be more hands-on with your patient when you're a veterinary technician. You spend a lot of time with your patient as a veterinary technician, which I was like, oh, I love that. What is your favorite part of this job? My favorite part of my job right now is coming in and seeing my team ready to go. Nothing makes me happier to have a very supportive team to not only have my back and each other's back, but to have so much passion and love for what they do. It really makes a difference. What was your experience like at Foothill College? Foothill was amazing. The teachers that I have, they really care. Sorry, I miss them. So I'm like, starting to tear up. Every single one of those teachers from the program director to the part-time staff, they're amazing. Even when they're not at school working, if you send them an email for questions, they'll answer you back on their days off. So you're a first generation college student and that's a huge accomplishment. What does it mean to you? For me, it was a lot of pressure just because my mom her and her family, they escaped the Vietnam war, they came here by boat and they struggled to make a living and, you know, to give their children the best life that they can give. So me being first generation college student, I wanted to make sure that I can make my mom proud and that she knows like all of her hard work, didn't go to waste. Were there any struggles that came with being the first in your family to go to college? Yeah. It was just a process of like knowing what steps I needed to do to get to where I want to be, was just really hard because my family is like, go to college, get a job. Okay, what are the steps in between? They're like I don't know, figure it out. I'm like, oh great. Can you describe a person who would be great as a vet tech? Someone that's one, willing to learn. Not like, learn in school but like continue to learn at their job, outside of their job. They need to have a huge heart. They need to be motivated to wake up and have a positive attitude and be very patient and be very compassionate for, you know, themselves, of course, other people and their pets. Also great leadership, you know, they speak up when they see something is wrong. Take the initiative in doing things. Anyone can be a veterinary technician, as long as you have those traits, you will succeed in this field. Can we see your dogs? This is the first one. Her name is Momo. This is my ten-month-old corgi. Her name is Rina Melody. This one's Hermione. She was abandoned in a box inside PetSmart. So cute, so so precious. Aileen, thank you so much for sharing your story with us today. It has been such a pleasure. Yeah, thank you. I'm Katelyn. And this was Gladeo on the Go, Zoom Edition with Aileen, the vet tech. We will see you next time.

title: Meet Jeanette, Environmental Engineer
transcript: Mathematical mind, flare for tinkering, curiosity... but what makes an engineer? My name is Jeanette and I'm an environmental engineer. Society is always going to have problems and engineers are the group of people that will get together and design something to address that problem. When you talk about an environmental engineer, that's somebody who's said, specifically, that they want to face problems that have to do with the environment, whether it be air, water, soil. As a kid, I always liked to be outdoors. I really liked planets, solar systems... things like that collecting rocks... Which is funny now that I'm an environmental engineer because it's really important to know the soil underneath we're standing on and what it does. When I was a freshman in high school, there wasn't a big, I would say, push to go to college, necessarily. It wasn't a hot topic but there was something unique about my class that we just kind of were always looking out for each other. There was some special bond that we had, that was a positive like motivating force. By the time I became a senior, it wasn't like if you were going to go, it was where are you gonna go? In high school, I found that I was really good at the math classes and the sciences, but I struggled through my English courses, writing, doing book assignments and turning in those papers. My geometry teacher just asked what are you going to go and study when you get to college? He was the one that actually saw something in me that I didn't even see in myself. I was wondering why do people get sick and what in the environment might cause that and he was the one that suggested to look into engineering. I had no idea what engineering was. My impression of an engineer was a train conductor like somebody, you know, making sure the trains arrived on schedule. When I went into college, a professor from the school of environmental engineering came and gave a couple of lectures. He specifically was working on a research project that was tying in air pollution to and correlating it with the development of asthma in children. And as soon as he gave that lecture, I was just like sold. I was like, that's what I want to do. My father was diagnosed with colon cancer at the, he was 35 when he was diagnosed. At that time, I was still in junior high school. I felt like I had to grow up really quickly and understanding what cancer was and what it does to the body and what it can do to a family. My parents are really proud that I'm an engineer. I don't think they fully understand what that is though. Nobody in my family has engineering background. I am the first in my family to go to college. When I started my freshman year at USC, it was a culture shock, not just because everyone looked different, but everyone also had different experiences. In the classroom, I was finding myself competing with people who went to private school who had had private tutors in the past. Didn't look like me, didn't talk like me and didn't have the same expectations for their lives like I did either. I ended up finding a good group of people to build a community with, and just kind of navigate that first year together. I feel like it was really necessary for me to go through that because it built character. I think everything in life has its purpose and its place. My biggest fear growing up is the fear of failing, and I think that kept me from, sometimes, even asking for help. Growing up, I just wanted to do it on my own and that way, if I failed, I would know, I would only know myself and nobody else would know and it's really, it's not a good way of growing. Everyone makes mistakes. If I could give my 13 year old self the greatest advice, I would tell her to stop worrying so much, to stop being afraid and just to go out and try new things and talk to new people and have different experiences. Whatever you want to be in life, I think you should try and find someone who's doing it and and just probe them and ask them questions and how they got there. More than likely, they're willing to share. I think my greatest hope growing up is still the same for me today. I'm still growing up... was just to make a difference in this world. That whatever I did or put my hands on or was a part of that it would make a positive impact. I am who I am and I don't focus too much on what I look like on the outside as opposed to what I know, what I can learn, and what I want to become. And the one certain spirited characteristic of the human heart, courage to accept constant challenge. The challenge to solve problems which perplex. Challenge to change the world into a better place. Challenge makes an engineer.

title: Meet Selena Castro, Director of Partnerships, Edlight
transcript: ﻿1 Hey, what's up everyone? I'm Courtney Echerd here with Gladeo on the Go and today I have the pleasure of sitting down with Vikki, a woman who is truly, always always on the go. You never stop and I just wanted to kick this off with saying, you know what is a job that you thought you would have right now 15 years ago. When I was about 15, I think I thought I was going to be the next Steven Spielberg. I was going to be a big famous Hollywood director. And then I realized there's all these other cool jobs in between that definitely much better suited my skills. Right now, what is the job you have? Tell us a little bit about your company. Yeah, so the job I have right now is Director of Business Affairs, and I work for a company called Omaze and we do really cool things to help raise money for nonprofits. We make people's dreams come true while also raising money for nonprofits. Yeah and I know that combines a couple of different passions of yours. You know, you got entertainment and music and law so I'd like to know a little bit about your history and how music kind of bled into law to create this job for you. So when I was 15, I was completely obsessed with movies and music. There's nothing that touched me more, and that I wanted to be a part of. So then I went on and I realized when I had dropped out of college, when I was 19 to manage fans, I'm negotiating a record label deal, and I realized, I'm in way over my head with these big, New York City attorneys. And then I realized, I want to be them. I want to be able to hold my own in deal-making for entertainment. So, that's what led me to law school and then I'm able to take that law degree and be an advocate for the creative. And then here at Omaze, I can make sure they were doing these really fair deals when we're making deals with musicians or entertainers and everyone in between to help make these dreams come true. I'm able to make sure that there's fair deals are happening and everyone feels good. Yeah, so dropping out of college at 19, how did you make your way back into law school? How did you make that transition? And it was fantastic because I dropped out because I was obsessed with entertainment and I said, why am I sitting in the classroom when I'm obsessed with entertainment and it was amazing because it was hearing that phone call and negotiating and I realized that there's so much more to learn that I wanted to learn and I figured out that going back to school and I got a full-time job at NYU, so I can go to school at night for free. I got my degree that way and I came out to California for Pepperdine Law school and I just realized that education was going to open a lot more doors for me. The hustle is a hustle. I did many many jobs along the way that were not what I was looking for, but education opened to so many more doors to finally combine all those passions in the best way possible. Totally. So I know that you are nowhere near done that you've got a whole life to live and that this is not the end. So what would your next dream be? What direction do we want to move in? So, I've spent my life working for other people. and the honest answer is, I love for Omaze to be my last job working for someone else. I've helped lots of people execute their vision. I'm doing the work right now to figure out what's my vision and how do I put it out there and I'm open to seeing what form that comes in right now. Omaze is a great job right now. We've raised over a hundred million dollars for charities and I'm so proud to be part of this place. But it also allowed me to do the work to figure out what my vision is. It sounds like you are in the perfect place to do that. So thank you so much for sitting down with me. I really appreciate it again. I'm Courtney Echerd and this is Vikki Karan And we are here on Gladeo on the Go. Thanks for tuning in guys.

title: Online Mentorship and Learning Platforms
transcript: Online Mentorship and Learning Platforms Beyond volunteering and apprenticeships, there are online mentorship programs and learning platforms designed to help you build valuable skills while connecting with industry professionals. Big Brothers Big Sisters eMentoring offers mentorship programs for young professionals. LinkedIn Career Advice: connect with experienced professionals for career guidance and mentorship. Coursera for Campus and Udacity Nanodegrees: Both platforms provide certification courses in high demand fields, helping you upskill for the job market.

title: Disclaimer Video
transcript: Important note, students are not required to create LinkedIn accounts. If you have an account, you're not required to upload any materials or anything you write for these knowledge checks. Uploading anything to your LinkedIn account is a personal choice. If you decide to upload your writing, please have it reviewed by your teacher, parent, or guardian first. You must be 16 years or older to make a LinkedIn account. If you're under 16, please wait until you're eligible before signing up.

title: It's All in the Details
transcript: ﻿1 It's All in the Details! Before you hit send, take a moment to proofread your cover letter. One typo can make a bad impression, so double-check everything. If you're submitting it online, save it as a PDF to make sure the formatting stays clean. At the end of the day, a cover letter is your chance to get noticed. It lets you explain why you're excited about the job and why you'd be a great fit for the organization. Don't stress too much about it. Just be yourself. Put in the effort and show the employer why you're worth meeting. Your cover letter could be the key to landing that interview and getting one step closer to your dream job!

title: What is a Portfolio?
transcript: ﻿1 What should be in your portfolio? Portfolios aren't a one size fits all collection. The content and structure should reflect the type of role you're aiming for. Here's what you should focus on depending on your field: For creatives. If you're in a creative field, your portfolio should reflect the full spectrum of your talents. For visual artists, like graphic designers, fashion designers or photographers include everything from illustrations, mockups, logo designs, sketches, and completed photo shoots. Voiceover artists, sound designers, and composers might include demo reels, folio work, or specific soundscapes. For advanced visual work, such as VFX, artists, animators, or sculptors, showcase your best 3D renders, animations, character models, or sculptures. For tech roles. For tech roles like front-end engineers or software developers, employers expect to see technical documents, app mockups, or even functional code snippets. Highlight your problem-solving skills with screenshots of app interfaces. You've designed, wire frames for websites, and snippets of your clean, organized code. For writers or marketers. If your skillset leans towards writing or digital marketing, your portfolio should showcase your versatility in creating engaging content. Include samples like blog posts, social media campaigns, ad copy, or even long-form articles. Clear labeling. Regardless of your field, you should title each project and include a short description. These captions should focus on what the project was, the challenge you faced, and what you achieved. The goal is to provide evidence of your talents through diverse examples while presenting a narrative about your creative process, technical skills, and adaptability.

title: AI and Its Effect on the Working World
transcript: AI and Its Effect on the Working World What is AI? Computer scientists have been developing artificial intelligence or AI for years, but what exactly is it? AI has been defined as a machine's ability to perform the cognitive functions we usually associate with human minds. In other words, AI is when a computer program can think like a person. That includes its ability to learn new things, solve problems, be creative, and talk to humans the way we talk to each other. AI is already used in thousands of apps and programs we use all the time. It's also used in hundreds of different career industries like banking, manufacturing, real estate, healthcare and more. There are different types of AI for different purposes. The newest type is called Theory of Mind, which can understand humans better and figure out what we need. Theory of mind includes artificial narrow intelligence like the voice assistant program, Siri and Alexa, and generative AI like Google's Bard and ChatGPT. There's another type of AI being worked on called Artificial general intelligence or strong AI. Strong AI would involve a computer program being self-aware and able to solve problems, learn and plan for the future, as IBM explains it. Strong AI doesn't exist yet, but if it ever does, it'll be as close to a human mind as possible. AI breakthroughs have changed our daily lives a lot already. They're even changing how we work. Let's explore how.

title: Bringing It All Together
transcript: ﻿1 Bringing It All Together. Reflecting and journaling are powerful tools for building resilience because they help you connect the dots between your experiences and your personal growth. The simple act of writing down your challenges and the steps you took to overcome them creates a narrative of strength. You can revisit whenever you feel stuck or uncertain. By tracking your progress over time, you begin to see how each setback teaches you something valuable, and how each success story adds another brick to your foundation of confidence. It doesn't matter if your entries are polished or messy, long-winded, or just a few bullet points. What matters is the habit of pausing, thinking about what happened, and noting what you can learn from it. Keep at it, and you'll discover that reflection isn't just an end-of-the-day exercise, it's a mindset that turns everyday ups and downs into building blocks for future success. So grab a notebook or open a fresh document and give journaling a try. You'll likely find that putting your experiences into words helps you process them more fully, and reminds you in real time that you're always learning, always growing, and always capable of bouncing back!

title: Accepting That Setbacks Are Normal
transcript: ﻿1 Accepting That Setbacks Are Normal. No matter how carefully you plan or how talented you are, life has its own pace. You might get a lower grade than you wanted, or miss out on an opportunity you thought was a sure thing. Rather than treating setbacks as proof you're not good enough, see them as a natural part of any journey. When something goes wrong, it can help to reflect on what happened. Ask yourself what you can learn from the situation - maybe you need more study time or a different approach. Remember it's temporary: Setbacks happen... BUT people bounce back from disappointments every day, and you can too! By viewing each stumble as a chance to learn and adapt, you train yourself to keep moving forward. You'll also realize that most successful people have a string of failures behind them - chances are, those setbacks actually led them to a better path.

title: Building Your LinkedIn Profile
transcript: Adding skills and recommendations to your LinkedIn profile. In a previous module, we talked about how including keywords in your profile helps potential employers find you. Another way to boost your visibility is by adding skills. You can add up to five skills for every employment period you add. Plus five skills for every educational experience. But what skills should you add?

title: Replacing Self-Criticism with Supportive Thoughts
transcript: ﻿1 Replacing Self-Criticism with Supportive Thoughts. Notice the language you use in your own head. If you are constantly telling yourself, "I'm terrible at everything," it reinforces the idea that you're not capable of growth. A more balanced approach might be, "I didn't do well this time, but I'm capable of improving if I keep practicing." A simple way to support this mindset shift is by keeping a few positive phrases in mind. For example, "I am learning and growing every day," or "I'm capable of achieving my goals." Repeating these statements can feel a bit awkward at first, but over time, they help override automatic negative thoughts.

title: What Self-Compassion Looks Like
transcript: ﻿1 What Self-Compassion Looks Like? Think of how you'd treat a friend who's feeling down: you'd reassure them offer to listen, and suggest a way forward. When you turn that same level of care inward, you create an emotional safety net that helps you recover from mistakes and keep going. Instead of piling on shame or guilt, you remind yourself that everyone stumbles sometimes. Self-compassion also fosters long-term growth more effectively than harsh self-judgment. It makes you more resilient because you are less afraid to fail; you know that at the end of the day, you'll treat yourself fairly if things don't go as planned. One. Mindfulness: Observing without judgment. Mindfulness is the practice of noticing your present thoughts and feelings without immediately labeling them as "good" or "bad." For example, if you are upset about scoring low on a test, mindfulness encourages you to pause and acknowledge the emotions - maybe you're embarrassed, worried about your grade, or frustrated that you studied hard but still didn't do well. Simply noting those feelings helps you respond more calmly instead of spiraling into self-blame. If you want to ease yourself into mindfulness, you might try a brief, daily check-in. Find a quiet moment: Sit comfortably and take a few slow deep breaths. Name your emotions: If you're sad, nervous, or stressed, identify what you're feeling without judging yourself for it. Refocus: Once you've acknowledged the feeling, gently bring your attention back to your breath or the present moment. By practicing mindfulness, you become more aware of what's really going on internally, and less likely to cling to negative thoughts as facts. Two. Common Humanity: You are not alone. It's easy to feel like you're the only one making mistakes, but that's never the case. Everyone experiences failure, frustration, or rejection at some point. It's part of the human experience. Recognizing this common humanity can be incredibly comforting and reduces the sense of isolation that often accompanies self-doubt. When you're feeling low about something, remind yourself that countless people have faced similar issues. Talking with friends, teachers, or family members about your struggles can help you see that you're not alone. If you realize a mentor you admire once struggled in the same way, you are more likely to cut yourself some slack. After all, they overcame it, which means you can, too. Three. Kindness to yourself. Kindness to yourself is about offering understanding and empathy in moments when you might otherwise feel embarrassed or guilty. Instead of telling yourself, "I should have done better," try shifting that language to something more compassionate, like, "I was nervous and made a mistake. It happens. I'll know what to do differently next time." Sometimes kindness also means taking practical steps to support yourself. If stress keeps piling up, give yourself permission to rest or do something fun, even if it's just a quick break. Avoid labeling these breaks as "lazy" moments; see them as essential recharging periods that help you stay mentally and emotionally balanced.

title: What Should Be in Your Portfolio?
transcript: ﻿1 What should be in your portfolio? Portfolios aren't a one size fits all collection. The content and structure should reflect the type of role you're aiming for. Here's what you should focus on depending on your field: For creatives. If you're in a creative field, your portfolio should reflect the full spectrum of your talents. For visual artists, like graphic designers, fashion designers or photographers include everything from illustrations, mockups, logo designs, sketches, and completed photo shoots. Voiceover artists, sound designers, and composers might include demo reels, folio work, or specific soundscapes. For advanced visual work, such as VFX, artists, animators, or sculptors, showcase your best 3D renders, animations, character models, or sculptures. For tech roles. For tech roles like front-end engineers or software developers, employers expect to see technical documents, app mockups, or even functional code snippets. Highlight your problem-solving skills with screenshots of app interfaces. You've designed, wire frames for websites, and snippets of your clean, organized code. For writers or marketers. If your skillset leans towards writing or digital marketing, your portfolio should showcase your versatility in creating engaging content. Include samples like blog posts, social media campaigns, ad copy, or even long-form articles. Clear labeling. Regardless of your field, you should title each project and include a short description. These captions should focus on what the project was, the challenge you faced, and what you achieved. The goal is to provide evidence of your talents through diverse examples while presenting a narrative about your creative process, technical skills, and adaptability.

title: How to Present Your Portfolio During an Interview
transcript: ﻿1 How to present your portfolio during an interviews A portfolio can be a powerful asset in interviews, whether the interviewer has seen it in advance or not. Here are tips to present it confidently: Bring extra copies. Determine how many people will be interviewing you and prepare enough copies for everyone, including one for yourself. This can be essential in panel interviews, which often involve multiple interviewers. Know your portfolio inside and out. Being familiar with the content and organization of your portfolio helps you present seamlessly. Avoid fumbling by clearly labeling each section, so you can quickly locate items relevant to questions asked. Start with a strong introduction. This brief "brand" introduction should capture the interviewer's interest immediately. For example: You could say, "This portfolio showcases my ability to solve design challenges and collaborate effectively with clients." Emphasize key projects. Select two to three impactful pieces most relevant to the role. Give a concise explanation for each, focusing on the challenge, your approach, and the results. This provides context for your abilities and expertise in a way that resonates with the role. Encourage interaction. Invite the interviewer to engage with specific pieces. For example: "Would you like to see more of the wire frames that show the evolution of this design?" This keeps the conversation dynamic and holds the interviewer's attention. End with a call to action. Wrap up with enthusiasm for the position and your eagerness to bring your skills to their team. A closing like, "I'm excited to discuss how my experience can add value to your team", reinforces your interest in the role. Creating a winning portfolio is about more than just gathering work samples. It's about telling a story. Your portfolio should give potential employers a window into your creative journey, technical skills, and the passion for what you do. Keep it updated, keep it organized, and make sure it reflects the best of who you are.

title: How to Structure and Organize a Portfolio
transcript: ﻿1 How to structure and organize a portfolio (with case studies) The way you organize your portfolio is as crucial as what you put into it. A well-structured portfolio allows the employer to quickly understand who you are and what you bring to the table. Here's how to do it effectively: Organized by theme. Keep all similar work together. For example, group your logo designs in one section, app designs in another, and blog posts in a separate section. This way your audience can explore the type of work they're most interested in without unnecessary distractions. Chronological order. If you're showcasing a skill that has grown over time, like graphic design or writing, consider presenting your work in the order it was created to show progression. Highlight strongest pieces first. Start with your most relevant and impressive work. Employers often skim through portfolios quickly, so grab their attention right away. Let's take a look at a few case studies for portfolios you might put together in different career paths. We'll start with a graphic designers portfolio. Case Study: Graphic Designer Portfolio. Let's imagine you're building a portfolio as a graphic designer. Your portfolio should include the following: Mockups and logos. Highlight polished versions of logos, posters, and branding projects. Illustrations. Display a range of illustrations, sketches, and finished artwork. For example, if you designed a logo for a student club, include both the initial sketch and the final digital version to show your creative journey. Minimalist layout. Include a clean, minimalist design to let your work take center stage. Keep the design clutter free and professional, allowing your projects to shine without distractions. Case Study: Mobile App, Front-end Engineer Portfolio. Front-end engineers build the parts of apps and websites that users see and interact with. They focus on things like layout, navigation, and visual elements. If you're interested in tech, here's what a front end engineering portfolio could include: Code snippets and app designs. Include brief annotated snippets of well-documented code and app UI mockups to showcase your programming and design skills. Problem-solving stories. Show how you tackle challenges during a project. For example: "When developing an e-commerce app prototype, I streamlined the loading speeds, cutting load times by 40% to improve user experience." App wire frames and prototypes. Display wire frames, and if possible, link to interactive prototypes to demonstrate technical skills. It's okay if you didn't understand all that techy talk yet, but this example could be helpful if you pursue a job in the tech industry. Case Study: Writer or Content Creator Portfolio. If you're focusing on writing, marketing, or content creation, your portfolio will showcase work that highlights your voice, messaging style, and ability to engage an audience. Here's how you might structure it: Writing samples. Include a selection of your best blog posts, social media campaigns, or articles. If you've done any research writing, that's a great addition too, as it shows analytical skills. Content campaigns. Describe projects where you created a themed content series or social media posts. Explain your approach to attracting an audience and the positive results, such as an increased engagement or follower growth. Brand tone examples. Showcase examples of content that demonstrate your ability to adapt to different voices or styles, especially if they match the industry you want to work in. Case Study: Architecture Portfolio. For an architecture-focused portfolio, you'll want to emphasize spatial planning, creativity, and attention to detail. This might include blueprints and sketches. Start with hand drawn or digital sketches, then show how they evolved into full blueprints. Project descriptions. Provide a brief overview of each project's purpose, any challenges you encountered, and how you design solutions. For instance, "This library project required space for both quiet study areas and interactive community spaces, which I achieved by designing a split level layout." 3D renderings and models. Include any 3D renderings models, or detailed photos of the final results, especially if you completed a school or community project.

title: How does AI Help Us?
transcript: How does AI help us? AI programs are efficient and helpful. They do boring, repetitive tasks so that we don't have to. This gives us more free time to focus on other things. AI can even keep us safer. For example, in car manufacturing, AI works together with industrial robots to build cars on assembly lines. They do the most dangerous tasks so humans don't have to. At home, school and work, AI-powered search engines help us find things on the internet. AI apps make customized shopping and social media recommendations for us by using computer programming called algorithms. Algorithms are instructions that tell the AI what to do so it can use data to make decisions. Algorithms can save lives too. They help medical professionals detect diseases in patients and offer healthcare plans, so patients get well faster. In business and finance, AI prevents fraud and cybersecurity threats. AI smart devices save energy and make life more convenient. AI also helps in fields like education, environmental protection, transportation, entertainment, you name it. But because AI is doing so much work, it's changing the work left for people to do Generative AI Have you ever heard of chatbots? These generative AI programs use natural language processing to write complex articles and essays. Chatbot platforms such as Open AI's ChatGPT, and Google's Bard have changed how people work and how companies do business. Related programs like Canva AI, Crayon and Dolly create works of art, while Jukebox, Musenet and Soundraw compose music. This exciting wave of technology is changing whole career fields. Employees in creative jobs will have to adapt and learn how to use AI tools because AI is starting to do their work. In some cases, AI might replace human workers entirely, but there will still be a need for workers who understand how to use and develop AI. Let's look at some career fields projected to shrink or grow because of AI.

title: Virtual Volunteering & Online Work-Based Learning
transcript: Virtual volunteering and Online Work-based Learning Looking to gain real world experience while helping others from the comfort of your home. Virtual volunteering could be the perfect way to start. By offering your skills to nonprofits in need, you can build your portfolio, gain professional experience, and even make a positive impact in your community all without leaving your house. Many organizations are actively seeking volunteers to help with various projects online. Whether you're skilled in design, writing, data analysis, or marketing, there are plenty of opportunities available. Plus volunteering virtually allows you to work with professionals from different industries, giving you a well-rounded experience in a real world environment. Here are some platforms that can connect you with virtual volunteering opportunities. Catchafire matches skilled professionals with nonprofits that need help with projects. Taproot Plus, a platform for providing pro bono consulting to nonprofits. VolunteerMatch offers a variety of virtual volunteer opportunities for different causes. UN Volunteers engage in global volunteerism to support UN organizations virtually. All you need is a computer with an internet connection.

title: How to Build a Portfolio Without Paid Tools
transcript: ﻿1 How to build a portfolio without paid tools. Building a professional-looking portfolio doesn't have to cost much, if anything. There are plenty of free tools that make it easy to showcase your work and creativity without a subscription. Let's explore some options: PDF-based Portfolios. One of the simplest ways to build a portfolio is by using tools like: Google Slides, PowerPoint, or even Google Docs. To create a structured, visually engaging portfolio. This is especially useful for beginners who want to organize school projects, volunteer work, or independent creations. In a professional format. You can include images, texts, and even interactive elements like hyperlinks. Physical Portfolios. For hands-on careers like photography, tattoo, artistry, or cabinet-making creating a physical portfolio can leave a strong impression. This old-school approach can be particularly effective in design, fashion, and other creative fields where tactile presentation is valued. Free Online platforms. There are several online platforms that allow you to build and showcase a professional portfolio for free: LinkedIn. Not just for networking. LinkedIn lets you upload projects and multimedia to your profile, showcasing your skills. Behance. Ideal for visual creatives like designers and photographers. Behance lets you create stunning project presentations. Wix or WordPress. These platforms offer free plans that let you build a customized portfolio website, perfect for showcasing work in tech, design, or marketing. Canva. With its user-friendly templates, Canva makes it easy to design a portfolio that looks polished and professional, whether for design, writing or marketing roles. YouTube or Vimeo. Both platforms allow you to upload video work or a video presentation for free. From there, you can embed your video into a portfolio using any of the tools above, or just let the video live on the platform. Leverage school projects. If you don't have client work to showcase, use school projects, volunteer work, or personal challenges to build your portfolio. For example: If you've designed a mockup for our website in class, include it as a sample. Or, if you coded an app as a personal project, show it off with screenshots and code snippets. Notion for Tech Portfolios. For tech roles like app developers or UX/UI designers. Notion offers a free, customizable space to build an interactive portfolio. You can organize code snippets, wire frames, and screenshots into clear sections. Making it easier for employers to navigate your work.

title: Presenting Your Portfolio Effectively
transcript: ﻿1 Presenting your portfolio effectively. A strong portfolio isn't just a collection of your work; it's a strategic presentation of your skills, talent, and potential. Here's what to focus on for an impactful portfolio: Keep it concise. Highlight only your top projects to keep viewers engaged. Choose quality over quantity. Maintain cohesive design. Ensure a visually consistent layout, grouping similar projects together for easy navigation. A clear, organized design helps direct attention to your best work. Update it regularly. Keep your portfolio relevant by showcasing recent projects that reflect current industry standards. Add project descriptions. A portfolio isn't just about visuals. Include a brief summary of each project: What you did, why you did it, and the results achieved. Tailor for the role. Customize your portfolio based on the job or client you're targeting. Highlight relevant skills and projects that align with their needs. Guide viewers with calls to action. Include prompts like "View More" or "Let's Connect" to encourage engagement. Showcase testimonials. Include brief endorsements or testimonials to strengthen credibility. Testimonials add social proof and show that others value your work.

title: Being Kind to Yourself
transcript: ﻿1 Being Kind to Yourself. Have you ever messed up on a test, forgotten a big assignment, or felt like you were letting yourself or others down in some way? In those moments, it's common to slip into negative thoughts like, "I'm not good enough" or "I'll never get this right." But that kind of self-criticism only makes a tough situation tougher. This is where self-compassion comes in. Practicing self-compassion means treating yourself kindly when things don't go as planned. It's about recognizing that setbacks and failures are a normal part of growth, and that you deserve understanding, patience, and forgiveness just as much as anyone else. In this lesson, we'll explore three main elements that can help you build self-compassion, mindfulness, common humanity, and kindness to yourself. We'll also look at ways to replace negative self-talk with more supportive thoughts, so you can bounce back stronger from any challenge.

title: Meet Steve - IT Administrator
transcript: ﻿1 Tôi là Katelyn và đây là Gladeo trên Go Zoom Edition. Hôm nay, chúng ta sẽ nói chuyện với Steve, một giám đốc CNTT của City of Mountain View. Steve đã hoàn thành chương trình chứng chỉ GIS của trường Cao đẳng Foothill, nơi anh ấy đạt được các kỹ năng, giúp thành phố Mountain View luôn dẫn đầu về quản lý thông tin. Xin chào, tôi là Steve Rodriguez. Tôi là giám đốc CNTT của City of Mountain View. Là giám đốc , tôi quản lý các hoạt động hàng ngày của thành phố. Đó là tất cả các hoạt động kỹ thuật từ điện thoại đến internet không dây , cơ sở hạ tầng back-end, cơ sở hạ tầng máy tính để bàn và cơ sở hạ tầng máy chủ. Bạn sẽ nói như thế nào rằng trường Cao đẳng Foothill đã giúp chuẩn bị cho bạn để trở thành vị trí mà bạn đang đảm nhiệm? Chắc chắn. Vì vậy, chương trình Foothill, cụ thể là , tôi đã đăng ký ở đó để nâng cao kỹ năng GIS của mình lên đến tốc độ. Chương trình Foothill thực sự đã làm rất tốt. Họ bắt đầu ngay từ đầu. Vì vậy, ai đó giống tôi, người có rất nhiều kỹ năng kỹ thuật, nhưng không thực sự hiểu thế giới của GIS, Tôi bước vào với đủ khả năng để học nhưng tôi thực sự không biết bắt đầu từ đâu. Và điều tôi thực sự thích về nó ngày hôm nay, chương trình Foothill GIS thực sự là họ đã xác định nó rõ ràng như thế nào ngay từ đầu, cho đến tận cùng, theo như kỳ vọng của bạn, kỳ vọng là gì trong lĩnh vực này, cách tất cả những thứ này hoạt động, và sau đó thực sự đi đến điểm bạn đang gặp gỡ các chuyên gia, bạn được nói chuyện với những người trong lĩnh vực này. Và sau đó là tiêu đề của dự án lớn hơn, thực sự tuyệt vời đối với chúng tôi, cho bạn cơ hội thực hiện tất cả những điều đó và thực sự học cách đăng ký và nhận được phê bình bởi không chỉ sinh viên, mà còn từ các nhân viên và nói , tốt, đây thực sự là những gì bạn nên làm, đây là cách nó hoạt động. Bạn có thể nói thêm một chút về công nghệ không gian địa lý không? Nó là gì? Và bạn làm gì trong thế giới đó? Chắc chắn. Một trong những nhiệm vụ của tôi là quản lý chương trình GIS cho thành phố Với một GIS tốt, bạn có thể nhập dữ liệu về hầu hết mọi thứ và sử dụng công cụ đó một cách rất khoa học để giúp mọi người hình dung dữ liệu. Có rất nhiều điều khác đối với số ngoài việc xác định vị trí của mọi thứ trong lòng đất hoặc xác định số lần cuối cùng cái cây được cắt tỉa. Nếu bạn có thể xem xét những thứ như, chẳng hạn, khả năng tiếp cận phương tiện giao thông. Đó là một điều to lớn mà chúng tôi đang cố gắng tìm ra ngay bây giờ và nếu không có khả năng tham chiếu không gian địa lý thông tin đó, thì không thể thực sự hình dung được. Bạn biết đấy, chúng tôi có thể cho bạn biết vị trí của tất cả những người có thẻ thư viện của bạn và nơi có thể bạn nên đưa xe điện vào các khu vực lân cận đó vì họ không có quyền truy cập giống như ai đó có thể hoặc chúng tôi có thể chỉ cho bạn những thứ như, à, một trong những ví dụ tuyệt vời mà tôi đã thấy là họ có thể vẽ tất cả cây ăn quả trong thành phố và sau đó sử dụng nó để nói với mọi người, "này, nếu bạn cần thêm thức ăn, đây là tất cả Là." Và điều gì đã thôi thúc bạn dấn thân vào lĩnh vực này? Ban đầu, điểm thu hút thực sự đối với CNTT là thực sự không có ngày , không có ngày nào giống nhau. Mỗi ngày, vấn đề là , bạn luôn có những gì đã lên kế hoạch, nhưng sau đó vấn đề là đối phó với bất cứ điều gì sắp xảy ra. Tôi là một nhà quản lý đang làm việc. Tôi làm việc với mọi người mỗi ngày. Tôi gặp mọi người mỗi ngày. Và đó là một phần lớn của điều đó thực sự thu hút tôi về lĩnh vực này là, nó không chỉ thực sự chơi với công nghệ nhiều như thực sự sử dụng, triển khai, và sau đó xem kết quả cuối cùng. Và phần tốt nhất trong công việc của bạn là gì? Bạn yêu thích điều gì nhất? Vì vậy, điều yêu thích của tôi là thực sự giải quyết vấn đề. Tôi luôn là một trong những đứa trẻ lớn lên thích các bài toán đố và thích số để tìm lời giải khi không có lời giải. Với công nghệ, chúng ta thực sự phải làm được điều đó Bạn biết đấy khi làm việc cho một thành phố tiến bộ như Mountain View, chúng tôi có cơ hội nhận được rất nhiều thách thức bởi vì chúng thực sự ở mức bậc nhất của những gì có thể làm được. Nhưng chúng tôi coi CNTT và chính phủ như một cơ hội để tăng hiệu quả, để thực sự chịu trách nhiệm với số tiền mà chúng tôi đã phải trả và sau đó quay lại và đưa ra những ý tưởng tốt hơn và cách thực hiện mọi việc trong một cách tiếp cận hiện đại hơn, trái ngược với kiểu theo kiểu giáo điều chỉ bỏ đi bất kỳ cách làm cũ nào.
